_note: THE BESTIARY — a planet's fauna GENOME as pure data. The address (rng_seed) is the planet; its environmental laws (air, gravity, heat, lushness) roll once at genesis, and every species' BODY PLAN is shaped by those laws through plain formulas: thin air breeds flyers, heavy gravity breeds low-slung striders, cold breeds fur, gravity caps size. Same seed = same laws = same creatures, forever — the fauna are part of the planet's history, not a random skin. Part indices (torso/head/legs/tail/pattern) pick renderer drawing styles; continuous stats (size/leglen/fur/hue) tune them. (Portrayal is VERSIONED: slot counts widened v2 — head 5→16, torso 5→12 — and legs/tail added as new registries; the genome is permanent, an address just resolves to a richer library.) The cascade generator is the whole trick: cosmos → planet (laws rolled as child_stats) → species (reading parent.law), one reveal.

generators._note: species: `index` IS the species id (guaranteed distinct); `gene` salts every trait so two planets with similar laws still grow different-looking life. Trait = golden-ratio hash of (species, gene), bent by the parent planet's laws.
